title: ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. alpinum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. alpinum, also known as the Alpine Adder's Tongue or Alpine Ophioglossum, is a rare subspecies of the Adder's Tongue fern, belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Europe, where it has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. alpinum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and act as an antiviral agent, among other uses. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, which are believed to possess medicinal properties that can be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
